title: Rural Energy for America Program (REAP)
eligibility: Agricultural producers and rural small businesses
description: Provides guaranteed loan financing and grant funding to agricultural producers and rural small businesses to purchase, install, and construct renewable energy systems or make energy efficiency improvements.

title: Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Phase I
eligibility: US small businesses with fewer than 500 employees
description: Supports scientific excellence and technological innovation through investment of federal research dollars in critical American priorities to build a strong national economy.

title: California Arts Council Creative California Communities
eligibility: Nonprofit arts organizations and local governments in California
description: Supports projects that integrate arts and culture into local community development planning and implementation, building stronger and more vibrant communities.

title: EDA Build to Scale Program
eligibility: Nonprofits, universities, and government entities
description: Accelerates the growth of regional innovation economies by funding programs that build the capacity of regional entrepreneurial and innovation ecosystems.

title: Texas Commission on Environmental Quality Clean Air Grant
eligibility: Texas-based businesses and municipalities
description: Funds projects that reduce air pollution from mobile and stationary sources, including fleet vehicle replacements, equipment upgrades, and clean energy transitions.

title: USDA Value-Added Producer Grant (VAPG)
eligibility: Independent agricultural producers and farmer cooperatives
description: Helps agricultural producers enter into value-added activities related to the processing and marketing of bio-based products, helping to create new products, expand markets, and increase producer income.

title: New York State Health Research Science Board Grant
eligibility: Researchers at New York State institutions
description: Supports cutting-edge health research projects in New York State, prioritizing studies that address health disparities and improve community health outcomes.

title: EPA Environmental Justice Collaborative Problem-Solving Grant
eligibility: Community-based nonprofit organizations
description: Provides financial assistance to community-based organizations to help address environmental and public health challenges in communities overburdened by pollution.

title: Florida SBDC Network Startup Grant
eligibility: Early-stage startups in Florida with fewer than 50 employees
description: Provides seed funding to promising early-stage businesses in Florida, with an emphasis on scalable technology ventures and businesses serving underserved communities.

title: HUD Choice Neighborhoods Implementation Grant
eligibility: Local governments and public housing authorities
description: Supports locally-driven strategies to address struggling neighborhoods with severely distressed public housing through a comprehensive approach to neighborhood transformation.

title: Illinois Clean Energy Community Foundation Grant
eligibility: Illinois nonprofits, schools, and local governments
description: Funds energy efficiency and renewable energy projects at schools, nonprofits, and government facilities across Illinois, reducing utility costs and carbon emissions.

title: NIH Small Research Grant Program (R03)
eligibility: Researchers at accredited US institutions
description: Supports small research projects that can be carried out in a short period of time with limited resources, including pilot and feasibility studies and secondary data analyses.

title: Colorado Office of Economic Development Advanced Industries Grant
eligibility: Colorado companies in advanced industries sectors
description: Accelerates growth and job creation in Colorado's advanced industries including aerospace, bioscience, electronics, energy/natural resources, infrastructure engineering, and technology.

title: USDA Community Facilities Direct Loan & Grant Program
eligibility: Rural communities with populations under 20,000
description: Provides affordable funding to develop essential community facilities in rural areas, including healthcare clinics, public safety facilities, community centers, and schools.

title: Washington State Arts Commission Project Support Grant
eligibility: Washington State nonprofit arts organizations
description: Supports arts organizations in delivering high-quality arts programming to the people of Washington State, with priority given to projects that serve diverse and underserved audiences.

title: DOT Better Utilizing Investments to Leverage Development (BUILD) Grant
eligibility: State, local, and tribal governments
description: Funds surface transportation infrastructure projects of local and regional significance, including roads, bridges, transit, rail, ports, and intermodal transportation projects.

title: Georgia Department of Agriculture Specialty Crop Block Grant
eligibility: Georgia agricultural producers and organizations
description: Enhances the competitiveness of specialty crops including fruits, vegetables, tree nuts, and nursery crops grown in Georgia through marketing, research, and promotion activities.

title: NSF Convergence Accelerator Program
eligibility: Multi-sector teams including academia, industry, and nonprofits
description: Accelerates use-inspired convergence research in areas of national importance by funding teams that can achieve transformational high-impact solutions within a three-year window.

title: Michigan Economic Development Corporation Business Development Grant
eligibility: Businesses creating jobs in Michigan
description: Supports businesses that are creating new jobs and investing capital in Michigan, with a focus on manufacturing, technology, and other high-impact sectors.

title: IMLS Museums for America Grant
eligibility: Museums of all disciplines across the US
description: Strengthens museums as active resources for lifelong learning and community engagement, funding projects that expand public access to collections and deliver high-quality educational programs.

title: Oregon Business Development Fund
eligibility: Oregon-based businesses creating or retaining jobs
description: Provides low-interest loans and grants to businesses in Oregon that are creating new jobs or retaining existing jobs, with a focus on traded-sector industries.

title: HRSA Rural Health Care Services Outreach Grant
eligibility: Rural health networks and community organizations
description: Expands the delivery of health care services to include new and enhanced services in rural areas, supporting partnerships between rural health care providers and community organizations.

title: Pennsylvania DCED Small Business First Program
eligibility: Pennsylvania small businesses with 100 or fewer employees
description: Provides low-interest loans and technical assistance to small businesses in Pennsylvania, supporting expansion, equipment purchases, and facility improvements that create new jobs.

title: FEMA Hazard Mitigation Grant Program
eligibility: State, tribal, and local governments
description: Helps communities implement long-term hazard mitigation measures after a major disaster declaration, reducing the loss of life and property from future disasters.

title: Minnesota Job Creation Fund
eligibility: Businesses creating at least 10 jobs in Minnesota
description: Provides performance-based grants to businesses that are expanding or locating in Minnesota, creating quality jobs and capital investment in the state.

title: USDA Distance Learning and Telemedicine Grant
eligibility: Rural schools, libraries, hospitals, and nonprofits
description: Helps rural communities use telecommunications to connect medical professionals, teachers, and librarians with patients, students, and patrons in distant rural areas.

title: North Carolina STEM Education Grant
eligibility: K-12 schools and nonprofits in North Carolina
description: Supports development and implementation of innovative STEM programs in North Carolina K-12 schools, encouraging student interest in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.

title: SBA 7(j) Management and Technical Assistance Program
eligibility: Small disadvantaged businesses and 8(a) program participants
description: Provides technical and management assistance to eligible small businesses through grants to service providers, helping businesses compete in the federal marketplace.

title: Virginia Tourism Corporation Marketing Leverage Grant
eligibility: Virginia tourism businesses and local governments
description: Leverages private and local investment in tourism marketing for Virginia, supporting regional tourism promotion campaigns that generate visitor spending and tax revenue.

title: DOE Solar Energy Technologies Office Incubator Award
eligibility: Companies with innovative solar technologies not yet commercially viable
description: Supports innovative solar energy technologies that address gaps in the current market, funding early-stage projects with the potential to significantly improve solar energy performance and reduce costs.

title: Arizona Commerce Authority Innovation Grant
eligibility: Arizona technology companies with fewer than 200 employees
description: Accelerates commercialization of innovative technologies developed by Arizona companies, providing funding for proof-of-concept work, prototype development, and market validation.

title: CDC Community Health Workers Training Grant
eligibility: State and local health departments and community organizations
description: Funds training and certification programs for community health workers who serve as liaisons between health services and communities, improving access to care in underserved areas.

title: Tennessee Department of Agriculture Agritourism Grant
eligibility: Tennessee farm operations offering agritourism activities
description: Supports development and promotion of agritourism activities on Tennessee farms, including farm stays, educational tours, corn mazes, pumpkin patches, and farm-to-table events.

title: NEA Our Town Community Development Grant
eligibility: Nonprofits partnering with local governments for arts-based placemaking
description: Supports creative placemaking projects that contribute to the livability of communities and help transform them into lively, beautiful, and sustainable places with arts at their core.

title: Wisconsin Economic Development Corporation General Business Development Grant
eligibility: Businesses expanding operations in Wisconsin
description: Provides financial assistance to businesses creating new jobs and investing in Wisconsin facilities and equipment, fostering economic growth throughout the state.

title: IMLS Library Services and Technology Act Grant
eligibility: State library agencies distributing funds to local libraries
description: Funds projects that use technology to enhance library services, expand digital access, support digital literacy, and improve collection quality for library patrons across the US.

title: Massachusetts Clean Energy Center Community Clean Energy Resilience Initiative
eligibility: Massachusetts municipalities and regional planning agencies
description: Helps Massachusetts communities develop and implement clean energy and energy resilience strategies, supporting solar, battery storage, and microgrid projects at critical facilities.

title: USDA ReConnect Broadband Grant
eligibility: Rural electric cooperatives, utilities, and municipalities
description: Funds construction of broadband infrastructure in rural areas that lack sufficient access to modern, high-speed internet service, enabling economic development and quality-of-life improvements.

title: Nevada Governor's Office of Economic Development Catalyst Fund
eligibility: Nevada businesses in high-growth industries
description: Provides grants to Nevada companies that are developing innovative products and services with significant potential for economic impact, job creation, and capital investment.

title: HHS Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Block Grant
eligibility: State agencies and nonprofit treatment providers
description: Funds substance abuse prevention, treatment, and recovery support services, helping communities address the opioid crisis and other substance use disorders through evidence-based interventions.

title: Iowa Economic Development Authority Workforce Training Grant
eligibility: Iowa businesses with training needs and job creation plans
description: Supports Iowa businesses in training new and existing employees, upgrading skills needed for expansion or diversification, and improving workplace efficiency and productivity.

title: DOL Apprenticeship Building America Grant
eligibility: Industry intermediaries, workforce boards, and training providers
description: Expands Registered Apprenticeship programs across industries, creating new career pathways in high-demand fields including construction, healthcare, technology, and advanced manufacturing.

title: Maryland Department of Housing Community Development Block Grant
eligibility: Maryland local governments and nonprofits in eligible areas
description: Supports a wide range of community development activities including affordable housing, public facilities, infrastructure improvements, and economic development in low- to moderate-income areas.

title: NOAA Sea Grant Marine Research Fellowship
eligibility: Universities and research institutions in coastal states
description: Supports applied marine research that improves the understanding and stewardship of US coastal and ocean resources, connecting science with management decisions.

title: Kentucky Agricultural Development Fund Grant
eligibility: Kentucky farmers, agribusinesses, and rural organizations
description: Diversifies and strengthens Kentucky agriculture by funding new opportunities for farmers, supporting value-added enterprises, and improving agricultural infrastructure across the Commonwealth.

title: FHWA Transportation Alternatives Program
eligibility: Local governments, MPOs, and nonprofits
description: Funds non-motorized transportation and infrastructure projects including pedestrian and bicycle facilities, scenic overlooks, historic preservation, and environmental mitigation.

title: Louisiana Office of Cultural Development Arts Grant
eligibility: Louisiana nonprofit arts organizations
description: Preserves and promotes Louisiana's unique cultural heritage through support for arts organizations presenting music, visual arts, theater, dance, and traditional crafts across the state.

title: HUD HOME Investment Partnerships Program
eligibility: State and local governments
description: The largest federal block grant for affordable housing, funding construction and rehabilitation of rental housing and homeownership assistance for low-income families.

title: Alabama Department of Commerce Site Development Grant
eligibility: Alabama local governments and industrial development authorities
description: Prepares industrial sites in Alabama for business development by funding infrastructure improvements including roads, utilities, and grading, attracting new industries and jobs.

title: USDA Farm to School Grant Program
eligibility: Schools, nonprofits, and state agencies connecting school food programs with local farms
description: Improves the health of children by connecting schools with local farms, teaching students about agriculture, and incorporating local foods into school meal programs.

title: New Mexico Small Business Assistance Program
eligibility: New Mexico small businesses with fewer than 50 employees
description: Connects New Mexico small businesses with technical assistance from national laboratories, helping commercialize innovative technologies and improve business processes.

title: DOE Weatherization Assistance Program
eligibility: State energy offices distributing funds to local agencies
description: Reduces energy costs for low-income households by improving the energy efficiency of their homes, protecting health and safety while reducing energy burden.

title: Mississippi Development Authority Tourism Grant
eligibility: Mississippi local governments and tourism organizations
description: Supports tourism development and promotion in Mississippi, funding marketing campaigns, event support, heritage tourism projects, and cultural attraction improvements.

title: NSF Historically Black Colleges and Universities Excellence in Research
eligibility: Researchers at Historically Black Colleges and Universities
description: Builds research capacity at HBCUs by funding research projects that strengthen STEM disciplines, develop the research infrastructure, and increase the production of research-trained graduates.

title: Arkansas Economic Development Commission Quick Action Closing Fund
eligibility: Businesses locating or expanding in Arkansas
description: Provides flexible funding to attract and retain businesses that create quality jobs in Arkansas, offering a competitive edge when recruiting major employers.

title: West Virginia DHHR Behavioral Health Block Grant
eligibility: West Virginia licensed mental health and substance abuse providers
description: Funds mental health and substance use disorder treatment services in West Virginia, addressing the state's significant behavioral health challenges and expanding access to care.

title: EPA Brownfields Assessment and Cleanup Grants
eligibility: Local governments, tribes, and nonprofits owning brownfield sites
description: Funds assessment and cleanup of contaminated brownfield properties, enabling communities to reclaim idle industrial and commercial sites for productive reuse.

title: Hawaii Department of Business Economic Development Tourism Natural Energy Laboratory Grant
eligibility: Hawaii businesses and researchers in clean energy sectors
description: Supports development and commercialization of clean energy technologies in Hawaii, leveraging the state's unique natural resources including solar, wind, ocean thermal, and geothermal energy.

title: USDA Rural Business Development Grant
eligibility: Rural nonprofits, public bodies, and cooperatives
description: Supports targeted technical assistance, training, and other activities leading to the development or expansion of small and emerging private businesses in rural areas.

title: Alaska Denali Commission Rural Development Grant
eligibility: Alaska rural communities, tribes, and nonprofits
description: Strengthens rural Alaska communities through investments in essential infrastructure including energy systems, water and sanitation facilities, healthcare, and workforce development.

title: Ohio Third Frontier Technology Validation and Start-up Fund
eligibility: Ohio early-stage technology companies
description: Provides gap funding to help early-stage Ohio technology companies advance promising innovations from concept to commercialization, bridging the gap between research and market.

title: USDA Beginning Farmer and Rancher Development Program
eligibility: Nonprofits, universities, and extension services training new farmers
description: Develops and funds educational training, outreach, mentoring, and technical assistance initiatives for beginning farmers and ranchers, strengthening the next generation of agricultural producers.

title: Indiana Economic Development Corporation Regional Economic Acceleration and Development Initiative
eligibility: Regional development organizations and economic development entities in Indiana
description: Funds comprehensive regional economic development plans in Indiana that leverage local assets to attract investment, create jobs, and improve quality of life across multiple communities.

title: NEH Preservation and Access Research and Development Grant
eligibility: Universities, libraries, archives, and nonprofits
description: Supports projects that address challenges in preserving or providing access to humanities materials, developing solutions that have broad applicability across the preservation and archival community.

title: Kansas Rural Opportunity Zone Program
eligibility: Individuals relocating to designated Kansas rural counties
description: Attracts new residents to rural Kansas counties by offering student loan repayment assistance and income tax waivers to people who relocate and establish residency in designated counties.

title: SAMHSA Mental Health First Aid Training Grant
eligibility: State agencies, local governments, and community organizations
description: Trains community members to identify, understand, and respond to signs of mental illness and substance use disorders, building the capacity of communities to support people in mental health crises.

title: Montana Department of Commerce Big Sky Economic Development Trust Fund
eligibility: Montana businesses creating net new jobs
description: Provides grant assistance to expanding or relocating Montana businesses that are creating primary jobs and investing in Montana communities, supporting the state's rural economy.

title: DOE Industrial Efficiency and Decarbonization Office Grant
eligibility: Industrial manufacturers and research institutions
description: Accelerates development and deployment of technologies that reduce energy use and carbon emissions in energy-intensive industries including steel, cement, chemicals, and paper.

title: Nebraska Rural Development Commission Grant
eligibility: Nebraska rural communities and nonprofits
description: Strengthens Nebraska's rural communities by funding projects that improve quality of life, build local capacity, support agriculture, and stimulate economic activity in small towns.

title: HUD Continuum of Care Homeless Assistance Grant
eligibility: Nonprofits and local governments addressing homelessness
description: Funds a broad range of projects assisting homeless individuals and families, including transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, rapid rehousing, and homelessness prevention services.

title: South Carolina Research Authority Applied Research Grant
eligibility: South Carolina companies and research institutions
description: Bridges the gap between applied research and commercial product development for South Carolina companies, funding projects with clear pathways to marketable products and job creation.

title: USDA Organic Agriculture Research and Extension Initiative
eligibility: Land-grant universities, research institutions, and nonprofits
description: Funds research, education, and extension projects that address key challenges of organic agriculture production and marketing, advancing the transition to sustainable farming practices.

title: Utah Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity Film Grant
eligibility: Film and television productions filming substantially in Utah
description: Attracts film, television, and digital media productions to Utah by offering production grants tied to in-state spending, supporting the creative economy and generating tourism.

title: HRSA Health Center Program Look-Alike Designation
eligibility: Nonprofit organizations seeking federal health center designation
description: Supports organizations that provide comprehensive primary care services to underserved populations regardless of ability to pay, expanding access to affordable healthcare in medically underserved areas.

title: Oklahoma Department of Agriculture Food and Forestry Rural Small Business Grant
eligibility: Oklahoma rural small businesses with fewer than 25 employees
description: Supports rural small businesses in Oklahoma with funding for equipment, facility improvements, and marketing, helping entrepreneurs in agricultural and food-related industries grow their operations.

title: NSF Computer and Information Science and Engineering Research Initiation Initiative
eligibility: Early-career faculty at accredited US universities
description: Provides startup research funding to faculty in early stages of their academic careers, supporting research in computer and information science, networking, and cybersecurity.

title: Missouri Department of Economic Development Targeted Industrial Manufacturing Enhancement Program
eligibility: Missouri manufacturers investing in facility or equipment upgrades
description: Supports Missouri manufacturers through grants for equipment and facility upgrades that increase productivity, create new jobs, and maintain the competitiveness of the state's manufacturing sector.

title: USDA Rural Housing Repair Loans and Grants
eligibility: Very low-income rural homeowners aged 62 or older
description: Provides loans and grants to very low-income rural homeowners to repair, improve, or modernize their homes or to remove health and safety hazards, preserving affordable rural housing.

title: Wyoming Business Council Main Street Business Committed to Excellence Grant
eligibility: Wyoming downtown businesses and Main Street organizations
description: Strengthens Wyoming's downtown districts by supporting local business development, storefront improvements, marketing, and programming that drives foot traffic and economic activity.

title: DOJ Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant
eligibility: State and local law enforcement and criminal justice agencies
description: Funds a broad range of criminal justice activities including law enforcement, prosecution, prevention and education, corrections, courts, and crime victim and witness programs.

title: Idaho Department of Commerce Rural Community Block Grant
eligibility: Idaho cities and counties with populations under 50,000
description: Funds infrastructure improvements and community development activities in rural Idaho communities, including water and sewer systems, streets, and public facilities.

title: USDA Sustainable Agriculture Research and Education Grant
eligibility: Farmers, researchers, and educators
description: Advances sustainable agriculture by funding on-farm research and education, farmer-to-farmer networking, and professional development that helps producers transition to more sustainable practices.

title: North Dakota Department of Commerce Economic Development Grant
eligibility: North Dakota businesses and economic development organizations
description: Supports economic growth in North Dakota by funding business expansion, workforce development, and infrastructure improvements that diversify and strengthen the state's economy.

title: ACFA Community Food Projects Competitive Grant
eligibility: Nonprofits and community organizations with food assistance programs
description: Supports the development of community-based food projects that promote self-sufficiency and food security among low-income populations, including food banks, community gardens, and nutrition programs.

title: South Dakota Governor's Office of Economic Development Future Fund
eligibility: Businesses creating jobs or investing in South Dakota
description: Provides flexible grant funding to attract new businesses and support the expansion of existing South Dakota companies, with a focus on primary sector employers creating above-average wage jobs.

title: Maine Technology Institute Seed Grant
eligibility: Maine technology companies with fewer than 50 employees
description: Provides early-stage funding to Maine companies developing innovative technology products and services, supporting proof-of-concept work and helping entrepreneurs validate their business models.

title: Vermont Agency of Agriculture Food and Markets Specialty Crop Grant
eligibility: Vermont farmers and agricultural organizations
description: Enhances the competitiveness of Vermont specialty crops through projects that improve marketing, research, food safety, pest and disease management, and sustainable production practices.

title: DOT Rebuilding American Infrastructure with Sustainability and Equity (RAISE) Grant
eligibility: State, local, and tribal governments and transit agencies
description: Funds road, rail, transit, and port projects that improve safety, environmental sustainability, quality of life, and economic competitiveness for urban and rural communities.

title: New Hampshire Division of Economic Development Workforce Training Fund
eligibility: New Hampshire businesses investing in employee training
description: Helps New Hampshire businesses invest in their workforce through customized training programs, improving productivity, increasing wages, and strengthening the state's competitive workforce.

title: Rhode Island Commerce Corporation Qualified Jobs Incentive Grant
eligibility: Businesses creating at least 10 new full-time jobs in Rhode Island
description: Attracts and retains businesses in Rhode Island by offering per-job incentive grants to companies that create new, quality full-time jobs at or above the state's average wage.

title: Delaware Division of Small Business EDGE Grant
eligibility: Delaware small businesses owned by minorities, women, veterans, or persons with disabilities
description: Supports the growth of historically underserved small businesses in Delaware through grants for equipment, marketing, professional services, and other business development expenses.

title: Connecticut Department of Economic and Community Development Manufacturing Innovation Fund
eligibility: Connecticut manufacturers with at least 10 employees
description: Supports Connecticut manufacturers in adopting advanced manufacturing technologies, improving productivity, expanding capacity, and creating new jobs through process and product innovation.

title: New Jersey Economic Development Authority Innovation Evergreen Fund
eligibility: New Jersey technology companies in seed or Series A stage
description: Catalyzes the growth of New Jersey's innovation economy by co-investing in early-stage technology companies, leveraging private capital and building the state's startup ecosystem.

title: USDA Wood Innovations Grant
eligibility: Businesses, nonprofits, and universities in wood products sectors
description: Expands uses for, and markets for, wood products from National Forest System and other forest lands, supporting forest health, economic development in rural timber communities.

title: HRSA Maternal and Child Health Services Block Grant
eligibility: State health agencies
description: Improves the health of mothers, infants, children, and adolescents, including children with special health care needs, through funding for preventive and primary care services.

title: EPA Green Infrastructure Municipal Assistance Grant
eligibility: Municipalities and utilities managing stormwater
description: Supports municipalities in developing and implementing green infrastructure approaches for managing stormwater, improving water quality, and creating community amenities in urban areas.

title: NSF Cyber-Physical Systems Program Grant
eligibility: Research teams at US universities and national laboratories
description: Funds foundational research in cyber-physical systems where computational and physical components are tightly integrated, advancing applications in healthcare, transportation, energy, and manufacturing.

title: DOE Tribal Energy Development Capacity Grant
eligibility: Federally recognized Tribal Nations and Alaska Native Villages
description: Builds capacity in Native American communities to develop, finance, and manage energy resources on tribal lands, supporting energy sovereignty and economic development.

title: AmeriCorps State and National Program Grant
eligibility: Nonprofits, government agencies, and faith-based organizations
description: Engages Americans in national service by funding organizations that place AmeriCorps members in high-need communities to address issues including education, poverty, disaster relief, and environmental stewardship.

title: SBA Women's Business Center Grant
eligibility: Nonprofit organizations operating Women's Business Centers
description: Funds nonprofit organizations to operate Women's Business Centers that provide comprehensive business training, counseling, mentoring, and technical assistance to women entrepreneurs across the US.
